description:
The tile overlay QML can trigger rendering of zero-dimension items if visible: true is set before geometry is computed. The guard in updateTileOverlayGeometry() (if tileRect.width() < 1 || ... continue) handles most cases, but the QML view->show() call can race ahead of geometry validation.

FIX: Defer view->show() until after geometry is validated (non-zero width and height confirmed).

FILES: src/effects/desktopgrid/desktopgrid.cpp (updateTileOverlayGeometry and tile overlay creation)

TESTING: Add/remove desktops rapidly while desktop grid is active, verify no CreatePixmap BadValue X11 errors in kwin log.
title: Defer tile overlay show() until geometry validated (zero-dimension guard)

description: Root cause: patched kwin_x11 loaded system KWinX11Platform.so plugin instead of patched version. The 4 new pure virtual methods added to EffectsHandler in kwineffects.h shift the vtable, so the system plugin dispatches to wrong function addresses, causing SIGSEGV on startup and shutdown. Fix: set QT_PLUGIN_PATH in restart script to load plugins from ~/.local/kwin-dev/.
title: Fix SIGSEGV crash: system KWinX11Platform.so vtable mismatch

description:
## Summary
After a fresh restart, plasmashell is snappy. As soon as the user switches KDE Activity, it completely stalls and becomes unresponsive. Memory grows ~50MB per activity cycle.

## Environment
- KDE Plasma 5.24.7, KWin 5.24.7 (patched with PSW spatial workspaces)
- 2 monitors (DP-3 1680x1050, eDP-1 1920x1200)
- 5 KDE Activities: COD, KOM, RBT, READ, WRITE
- 10 virtual desktops in 3x4 spatial grid
- System under heavy memory pressure (14GB/23GB used, 10GB swap)

## Root Cause Analysis

### Containment Explosion
Plasmashell creates desktop containments for each activity x screen combination. After repeated kwin restarts (from earlier vtable crash debugging), the config accumulated 41 desktop containments with lastScreen values 0-9, despite only 2 physical monitors. 30 were phantom containments for non-existent screens 2-9. Each containment loads a full QML scene, folder view, file watcher, wallpaper renderer, thumbnail generator. At startup all 41 load simultaneously -> 543MB RSS at 22 seconds.

### Activity Switch Memory Growth
Measured via D-Bus activity switching:
- Round 1 (5 switches): +50MB total
- Round 2 (5 switches): +27MB more (containments already existed, still leaking)
- Total: +77MB across 10 activity switches

### _NET_DESKTOP_NAMES Off-by-One
X11 reports 11 desktop names for 10 desktops. Our removeVirtualDesktop() renumbers remaining desktops but does not clear the old slot from NETRootInfo. Stale name may cause plasmashell to create an extra containment.

### Per-Activity Spatial Layout Changes
Each activity has its own spatial map. When activity changes, updateSpatialLayout() fires, _NET_DESKTOP_LAYOUT changes (grid dimensions may differ per activity), plasmashell potentially re-creates containments for new geometry.

## Partial Fix Applied
- Cleanup script removes containments with lastScreen >= monitor count
- Added to ~/scripts/restart_plasma_kwin.sh
- Reduced containments from 41 to 12, but baseline RSS still ~550MB
- Activity switch growth reduced but not eliminated (~3-15MB per switch)

## Key Data
- Idle RSS growth: zero (32kB/10s)
- Activity-driven growth: 3-15MB per switch, never freed
- Heap: 304MB of 554MB RSS
- Config: ~/.config/plasma-org.kde.plasma.desktop-appletsrc

## Files Involved
- src/virtualdesktops.cpp: removeVirtualDesktop() stale name (line 1019-1024)
- src/virtualdesktops.cpp: updateSpatialLayout() changes layout on activity switch (line 1478)
- src/virtualdesktops.cpp: updateLayout() spatial mode path (line 1182-1193)
title: Plasmashell stalls on activity change with PSW spatial desktops

description:
## Problem
Commit 1c6b1a3 removed child MouseArea hover checks from the `tileHovered` binding in tile_overlay.qml (lines 19-24) to fix a binding loop. This causes flicker: when mouse moves to a button region, hoverArea.containsMouse may go false, tileHovered becomes false, buttons disappear, mouse is over empty space, hoverArea becomes true again — repeat.

## Previous working version (commit 96c0fc3)
```qml
property bool tileHovered: hoverArea.containsMouse
                           || closeHover.containsMouse
                           || aboveHover.containsMouse
                           || belowHover.containsMouse
                           || leftHover.containsMouse
                           || rightHover.containsMouse
```

## Current broken version (HEAD)
```qml
property bool tileHovered: hoverArea.containsMouse
```

## Root cause
The binding loop was: tileHovered → button visible → MouseArea geometry → containsMouse → tileHovered. The fix removed all child checks, which broke hover tracking.

## Fix approach
Restore the aggregated hover check from 96c0fc3. The binding loop was caused by visibility toggling geometry, not by reading containsMouse. To break the loop: keep buttons' MouseAreas always instantiated (don't gate their existence on tileHovered), only gate their `visible` property. Alternatively, use `opacity: 0` instead of `visible: false` for buttons so geometry doesn't change.

## File
src/effects/desktopgrid/tile_overlay.qml — lines 19-24 (tileHovered), lines 80, 105, 120, 135, 150 (button visibility)
title: Fix icon flicker regression in tile overlay hover detection

description:
PRIMARY cause of plasmashell stall on activity switch.

PROBLEM: updateSpatialLayout() (virtualdesktops.cpp:1404-1480) recomputes grid dimensions from the current activity's spatial map and calls m_rootInfo->setDesktopLayout(columns, rows). If Activity A has 2x3 and Activity B has 3x4, every switch changes _NET_DESKTOP_LAYOUT. Plasmashell reacts to each layout change by reallocating containment widgets, leaking ~10-15MB per switch.

FIX: Compute a fixed grid that accommodates ALL activities (max cols x max rows across all activity spatial maps). Only change _NET_DESKTOP_LAYOUT when desktops are added/removed, not on activity switch. The activity-change handler (line 613-627) should rebuild the internal grid (m_grid) without touching _NET_DESKTOP_LAYOUT.

FILES: src/virtualdesktops.cpp lines 613-627 (activity change handler), lines 1404-1480 (updateSpatialLayout), lines 1180-1193 (updateLayout spatial path)

TESTING: Switch activities 10 times, verify _NET_DESKTOP_LAYOUT stays constant (xprop -root _NET_DESKTOP_LAYOUT). Measure plasmashell RSS before/after.
title: Fix: use fixed grid dimensions across activities to prevent layout oscillation

This preserves the desktop for other activities while allowing per-activity grid customization.
title: Desktop delete in grid effect should be activity-aware
